user_story: As a returning user, I want to log in with email and password so that I can access my portal or admin dashboard
expected_behaviour: User enters email and password, clicks Sign in. On success, session cookie is set via /api/auth/verify, a welcome flash is queued, and the user is redirected to the last visited path under /portal or /admin (falling back to /portal/dashboard for clients and /admin/dashboard for admins). On wrong credentials, shows inline red error and a toast. Handles errors for wrong password, user not found, too many attempts, network failures, and session cookie failures. Toggle password visibility button available.

user_story: As a user who forgot their password, I want to request a password reset link from the login page so that I can regain access
expected_behaviour: Clicking Forgot? on the login page toggles to the Reset password panel. User enters email and clicks Send reset link. On success shows a green confirmation message and a Back to sign in button. On error shows inline red message. Handles user not found, too many attempts, network errors.

user_story: As a new user, I want to create an account with my name, email, and password so that I can access the client portal
expected_behaviour: User fills in full name, email, password, and confirm password fields. If passwords do not match, shows Passwords do not match. before any Firebase call. On successful registration redirects to /portal/dashboard. On error shows inline red message. Links to /login for existing users.

user_story: As an authenticated user visiting /portal, I want to be automatically redirected to the correct dashboard based on my role
expected_behaviour: Server component reads the session cookie and verifies it with Firebase Admin. If no session or verification fails, redirects to /login. If user is admin role, redirects to /admin/dashboard. All other roles redirect to /portal/dashboard.

user_story: As a user who received a magic link email, I want to click the link and be signed in automatically so that I can access a shared document or portal
expected_behaviour: Page receives ?customToken=X&redirect=Y query params. Uses signInWithCustomToken to sign in to Firebase, then exchanges the ID token for a session cookie via POST /api/v1/auth/session, then redirects to the redirect param. Shows a Signing you in... loading state. On missing token shows No sign-in token error message. On failure shows Sign-in failed with the error and a note that the link may have expired.

user_story: As a user who clicked a Firebase password reset email link, I want to be taken to a safe intermediary page before setting my password
expected_behaviour: Page reads ?link= query param and validates it against allowed Firebase hostnames and the /__/auth/action path. If valid, shows a Set my password button that links to the Firebase action URL. If invalid or missing, shows Link not valid message with a link to /login.

user_story: As a prospective client visiting the homepage, I want to see who Partners in Biz is, what they build, and how to get started
expected_behaviour: Page renders hero with headline, status indicator (Open for new work), and three CTA buttons (Start a project, See the work, Free growth audit). Bento grid shows featured case study tiles. Sections include: client logo marquee, partner-with-us banner, services grid, featured work alternating rows, process steps, tech stack pills, stats counters (CountUp animation), testimonials, pricing teaser, insights teaser, and FAQ accordion. All sections load without errors. CTA buttons link to correct destinations.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to learn about the studio's founder, philosophy, and engagement models so I can decide whether to work with them
expected_behaviour: Page renders founder section with photo and bio. Manifesto section lists five numbered principles. Stats section shows animated counters. Tech stack pill cloud renders. Behind-the-scenes image grid renders (images grayscale by default, colour on hover). Working-together section shows three engagement cards (Project, Retainer, Advisory) with pricing badges. Bottom CTA links to /start-a-project and cal.com booking URL.

user_story: As a visitor navigating to /contact, I want to be redirected to the booking page
expected_behaviour: Visiting /contact immediately server-side redirects to /book-a-call with no visible page content.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to see transparent pricing tiers, retainer options, and add-ons so I can evaluate the cost before reaching out
expected_behaviour: Page renders three pricing tier cards (Marketing Site from R35k, Web Application from R120k most popular, Bespoke Build). Web Application card has accent border and Most popular pill. Retainer cards show three levels with ZAR monthly pricing. Add-ons grid shows five items. Properties control plane callout renders. Currency note card renders. Pricing FAQ accordion renders with expandable items. CTAs link to /start-a-project and cal.com booking URL.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want answers to common questions about process, tech, and pricing in one place
expected_behaviour: Page renders four FAQ sections (General, Process, Technology, Pricing) each with a SectionHead and FAQ accordion. Each accordion item expands on click to reveal the answer. Bottom CTA card has links to /start-a-project and cal.com booking URL.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to browse all six services and understand the engagement models available
expected_behaviour: Page renders bento grid of six service cards (Web Development, Web Applications, Mobile Apps, AI Integration, Growth Systems, Bespoke Builds). Web Applications and AI Integration cards span two columns on desktop. Each card links to its detail page at /services/[slug]. Engagement modes section shows Project, Retainer, Advisory cards. FAQ accordion renders. CTAs link to /start-a-project and cal.com.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to read the full detail of a specific service including deliverables, process, pricing, and FAQs
expected_behaviour: Dynamic page resolves slug to one of six services. Renders breadcrumb (Home / Services / Name). Hero shows headline and intro. Properties callout renders for web-development, web-applications, and mobile-apps. Personas grid shows three target-client types. Deliverables grid shows 8-9 items. Four-step process grid renders. Tech stack pills render. Featured case studies show related work. Pricing band shows from price and included items. Service-specific FAQ accordion renders. Related insights section renders if available. Invalid slug returns 404.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to browse all case studies to evaluate the studio's track record
expected_behaviour: Page renders hero with headline and Currently building status strip. WorkGrid client component renders all case studies with filtering. CTA band at bottom links to /start-a-project and cal.com. Page load shows schema.org ItemList in document head.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to read a full case study including the brief, insight, build approach, outcomes, and deliverables
expected_behaviour: Dynamic page resolves slug to one of six case studies (velox, lumen, athleet, loyalty-plus, ahs-law, scrolledbrain). Renders hero with client name, industry, year, stack strip. Cover image renders. Four narrative sections render: The Brief (with pull quote), The Insight, The Build (with two visuals and build detail images), The Outcome (metrics grid and testimonial quote). Deliverables numbered list renders. Tech stack pills render. Next case link cycles to the next study. Bottom CTA links to /start-a-project. Invalid slug returns 404.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to browse all free SEO and growth tools available without signing up
expected_behaviour: Page renders hero with stat strip. Tool grid renders all tools from PUBLIC_TOOLS catalog as bento cards showing icon, category pill, title, description, output, and proof angle. Clicking any card navigates to /tools/[slug]. How-to-use section renders three-step guide. CTAs link to /start-a-project.

user_story: As a visitor, I want to use an individual free tool and see actionable results
expected_behaviour: Dynamic page resolves slug to a tool from the catalog. Renders breadcrumb. PublicToolInteractive client component renders the interactive tool UI. Tool-specific FAQ accordion renders. Invalid slug returns 404.
errors: Feature works correctly. Test slug was invalid; valid tool slugs return 200 with full tool detail page.

user_story: As a visitor, I want to browse all blog posts and insights from Partners in Biz
expected_behaviour: Page renders featured post as a large half-image half-text card. Remaining posts render in a three-column grid with cover image, category pill, title, description, date, and reading time. Category filter chips render. Newsletter subscription form renders at bottom. All post cards link to /insights/[slug].

user_story: As a visitor, I want to read a full blog post or build note
expected_behaviour: Dynamic page resolves slug first from Firestore (seo_content collection with status=live), then falls back to hardcoded POSTS array. Renders article with title, cover image, category, date, reading time, and full body content. schema.org Article markup in head. Invalid slug returns 404. Page revalidates every 60 seconds.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to book a 20-minute intro call with Peet directly from the website
expected_behaviour: Page renders heading Book a 20-min call, a brief description, a BookingWidget component (expected to embed or link to cal.com calendar), and a timezone note. Widget should allow time slot selection.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to submit a brief project description so Partners in Biz can respond with a scope
expected_behaviour: Page renders StartProjectForm component on the left. Right side shows what-happens-next steps, alternate contact methods (email, WhatsApp, cal.com), a testimonial card, and four trust badges. Form submission should send the brief and show a success or error state.

user_story: As a potential business partner, I want to see what venture opportunities are available and register my interest
expected_behaviour: Page renders hero with how-it-works 4-step strip. Three fit cards render. Ventures section renders all PARTNER_OPPORTUNITIES as article cards with icon, eyebrow badge, venture name, tagline, summary, stat pills, bullet points, and CTA linking to /partner-with-us/[opportunityId]. PartnerWithUsForm renders sticky on the right with the featured opportunity pre-selected.

user_story: As a potential partner, I want to read the full detail of a specific venture and apply through the form on the same page
expected_behaviour: Dynamic page resolves opportunityId to a PARTNER_OPPORTUNITIES entry. Renders hero with venture name, tagline, pitch, and stats strip. Sections: What is already built, Where you come in, What you get, Who this is for, Commercial conversation, Proof and review material, Sites and login details info cards, Expected next steps. PartnerWithUsForm renders sticky on the right pre-populated with the opportunity id. Invalid opportunityId returns 404.
errors: Feature works correctly. Test slug was invalid; valid partner opportunity IDs return full detail page.

user_story: As a prospective client, I want to learn about the Properties runtime control plane product
expected_behaviour: Page renders hero, feature grid (remote config, kill switch, feature flags, analytics, conversion sequences), before/after comparison for each feature, FAQ accordion, and CTA. All sections should render without errors.

user_story: As a client or stakeholder with a share link, I want to view a read-only SEO audit snapshot without logging in
expected_behaviour: Page looks up seo_audits by publicShareToken. On match, logs a view and renders: site name, site URL, day and capture timestamp, Traffic stats (impressions, clicks, avg position, CTR), Rankings breakdown (top 100/10/3), Authority stats (backlinks, referring domains), Content stats (pages indexed, posts published, comparison pages). On invalid/missing token returns 404.

user_story: As a Gauteng SME owner, I want to request a free growth audit to identify where my online enquiries are leaking
expected_behaviour: Page renders hero with problem statement and five leaks callouts. GautengGrowthAuditForm renders sticky on the right. Growth engine section shows three pillars. 90-day roadmap renders three phases. Local areas chip cloud renders. What-happens-next section renders four steps. FAQ accordion renders six questions. Submitting the form should capture business details and trigger a response.

user_story: As a buyer of a specific PiB product, I want to fill in a configuration form and submit to start onboarding
expected_behaviour: Dynamic page resolves product slug via getProduct(). Renders product name and description in header. AthleetOnboardingForm renders in the main two-thirds column. Sidebar shows pricing label, what is included feature list, turnaround note (3-5 business days), and a contact email. Invalid product slug returns 404.
errors: Feature works correctly. Test slug was invalid; correct slugs (athleet-management, loyalty-plus) return onboarding page.

user_story: As a visitor, I want to read Partners in Biz's privacy policy
expected_behaviour: Page renders a styled full-width legal document with header PRIVACY POLICY, last updated date (June 2026), and numbered sections covering information collected, how it is used, storage, third parties, cookies, rights, and contact details. Page renders without auth or redirects.

user_story: As a visitor, I want to read Partners in Biz's terms of service
expected_behaviour: Page renders a styled full-width legal document with header TERMS OF SERVICE, last updated date (June 2026), and numbered sections covering acceptance, services, payments, IP, disclaimers, and governing law. Page renders without auth or redirects.

user_story: As a user who loses their internet connection, I want to see a friendly offline page served from cache
expected_behaviour: Page renders when the service worker falls back to the offline shell. Shows headline We'll be back the moment your signal is., description of cache behaviour, a Try home page button linking to /, and a Retry button that calls location.reload().

user_story: As a client, I want to view a shared content campaign preview via a token link without logging in
expected_behaviour: Page looks up campaigns collection by shareToken where deleted=false. If shareEnabled is false or token not found, returns 404. Strips internal fields before rendering. Renders campaign name, master tagline, and AssetGrid in read-only mode showing social posts, blogs, and videos. Footer shows link to /portal to approve or schedule.

user_story: As a newsletter subscriber, I want to click the confirmation link in my email to verify my subscription
expected_behaviour: Page verifies the HMAC-signed token. On valid token: marks submission as confirmed (sets confirmedAt), runs performAutoEnroll, and renders a You're in! success message with the source name and confirmed email. If already confirmed: shows Already confirmed message. If token invalid/expired: shows Link expired or invalid message. Button to return to org site URL renders when available.

user_story: As an email subscriber, I want to update my topic preferences and email frequency from a link in an email
expected_behaviour: Page verifies HMAC token, loads contact and org config. Renders org-defined preferences page heading and subheading, the subscriber's email, topics checkboxes (transactional topic is permanently checked and disabled), frequency radio buttons (All, Weekly, Monthly, Transactional-only, Unsubscribe). On submit POSTs to /api/preferences/[token]. On success re-renders with ?saved=1 green confirmation banner. Invalid/expired token shows error message.

user_story: As a client who received a report link, I want to view a performance report for my organisation without logging in
expected_behaviour: Page looks up reports by publicToken. On first view, marks report as viewed. Renders ReportView component with brand name, period dates, and report data. Title shows org name and date range. robots meta set to noindex. Invalid token returns 404.

user_story: As a recipient of a shared document link, I want to read the published document without logging in
expected_behaviour: Page looks up client-documents by shareToken where shareEnabled=true, deleted=false, and latestPublishedVersionId is set. Loads the latest published version, strips private fields, and renders via DocumentRenderer. Returns 404 if token invalid, document deleted, sharing disabled, or no published version exists.

feature: Shared document edit view with access control
user_story: As a collaborator with an edit share link, I want to open a document in edit mode, with access code or sign-in required if configured
expected_behaviour: Client-side page calls GET /api/v1/public/client-documents/edit/[shareToken]. If 200 OK renders DocumentRenderer. If 401 with Code verification required shows AccessCodeForm. If 401 without code requirement shows SignInForm. If 410 shows This link has been disabled. If 404 shows Link not found.
errors: Returns HTTP 200 with client-side error state for invalid tokens. This is correct React app behavior — SSR 404 for invalid tokens is not required.

user_story: As a visitor on a client's website, I want to submit my email to subscribe via an embedded newsletter form in an iframe
expected_behaviour: Page serves inside an iframe at /embed/newsletter/[sourceId]. Loads capture source from Firestore. If source inactive renders This signup form is not active. Otherwise renders LeadCaptureEmbedForm with org-configured fields, theme, and success message. Form submits to /api/embed/newsletter/[sourceId]/submit. Invalid/deleted sourceId returns 404.

user_story: As a logged-in client user who attempted to access an admin route, I want to see a clear message explaining they do not have admin access
expected_behaviour: Page renders Admin access required heading, explains the account is a client portal account not a platform admin. Shows two buttons: Back to portal linking to /portal/dashboard and Sign in as admin linking to /login.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see all my CRM contacts in a paginated, searchable list so I can manage my audience
expected_behaviour: Page renders a table or card grid of contacts with columns: name, email, phone, tags, status, last activity. Search input filters in real time. Filter dropdown for tags and status. Pagination controls at bottom. New contact button in header. Each row/card is clickable, linking to /portal/crm/contacts/[id]. Empty state shown when no contacts.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to view and edit all details of a single contact including their activity history
expected_behaviour: Page renders contact name, email, phone, company, tags, notes, and status. Activity timeline shows emails sent, opens, clicks, form submissions. Edit button opens inline form or modal to update fields. Save POSTs to /api/v1/crm/contacts/[id]. Delete button asks confirmation then calls DELETE /api/v1/crm/contacts/[id] and redirects to contacts list. Invalid contact ID returns 404.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to manually add a new contact to my CRM
expected_behaviour: Form renders fields: first name (required), last name, email (required), phone, company, tags (multi-select), notes, status (Active/Unsubscribed/Bounced). Submit POSTs to /api/v1/crm/contacts. On success redirects to the new contact detail page. Inline validation for required fields and email format. Duplicate email shows error.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to import contacts from a CSV file to bulk-load my audience
expected_behaviour: ImportContactsModal or page renders file upload input accepting .csv files. On upload, preview shows first 5 rows with column mapping UI. User maps CSV columns to contact fields. Submit POSTs to /api/v1/crm/contacts/import. Progress indicator shown. On completion, shows count of imported, updated, and skipped records. Errors listed per row if any.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see all my audience segments to understand how contacts are grouped
expected_behaviour: Page renders list of segments with name, description, contact count, created date. Create segment button. Each segment links to detail/edit page. Delete button per segment with confirmation. Empty state shown when none exist.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to create a dynamic segment with rule-based filters to automatically group contacts
expected_behaviour: Segment builder renders name field and condition builder (AND/OR groups of rules). Each rule has field selector (email, tag, status, custom field, etc.), operator (contains, equals, is empty), and value input. Preview shows estimated contact count. Save POSTs to /api/v1/crm/segments. On success redirects to segment list. Invalid filter combinations show inline errors.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see all lead capture forms and sources I have created
expected_behaviour: Page renders list of capture sources with name, type (embed, form, manual), active status toggle, subscription count, and created date. New source button. Each source links to detail/edit. Embed code shown for embed-type sources. Toggle active/inactive POSTs to /api/v1/crm/capture-sources/[id].

user_story: As a portal user, I want to configure a new lead capture form including fields, double opt-in, and theme
expected_behaviour: Form renders: name, type selector, opt-in mode (single/double), success message, fields configurator (add/remove/reorder fields), theme options, tags to auto-apply to new leads. Submit POSTs to /api/v1/crm/capture-sources. On success redirects to source detail with embed code snippet.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to manage my contact tag library — create, rename, and delete tags
expected_behaviour: Page or modal renders all tags with name and contact count. Create tag input at top. Click tag to rename inline. Delete button removes tag after confirmation (warns about contact count). Changes sync immediately via API.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see my sales pipeline as a Kanban board showing deals in each stage
expected_behaviour: Board renders columns for each pipeline stage (Lead, Qualified, Proposal, Won, Lost by default). Each deal card shows contact name, value, and days in stage. Cards are draggable between columns (PATCH /api/v1/crm/deals/[id]). New deal button in each column opens a creation modal. Click card opens deal detail. Total value shown per column.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see all connected social media accounts and their connection status
expected_behaviour: Page renders card per connected account showing platform icon, account name/handle, status (Connected/Expired/Disconnected), and last synced time. Connect new account button per platform (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, TikTok). Disconnect button per account with confirmation. Reconnect button for expired tokens.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to connect my Facebook/Instagram account via OAuth so I can post and read analytics
expected_behaviour: Clicking Connect for Facebook opens /api/v1/social/auth/facebook which redirects to Facebook OAuth. On return, callback at /api/v1/social/auth/facebook/callback exchanges code for tokens, stores in social_accounts Firestore collection, and redirects back to /portal/social with success toast. On OAuth error shows error message.
errors: Facebook OAuth handled by generic /api/v1/social/oauth/[platform]/route.ts.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to compose a social media post once and publish or schedule it across multiple connected platforms
expected_behaviour: Composer modal or page renders platform selector checkboxes (only connected accounts shown). Rich text area with character counter per platform. Image/video upload. Schedule toggle: Publish now vs Schedule for date/time (datetime picker). Publish POSTs to /api/v1/social/posts. On success shows post in queue. Validation: at least one platform selected, no text exceeds character limit.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see all scheduled and published social posts in one view
expected_behaviour: Page renders list of posts sorted by scheduled_at. Each post shows platform icons, preview text, status (Scheduled/Published/Failed), scheduled time, and engagement metrics for published posts. Edit button for scheduled posts reopens composer. Delete button removes with confirmation. Retry button for failed posts.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see engagement metrics (impressions, reach, likes, shares, comments) across my social accounts
expected_behaviour: Page renders date range picker. Charts for impressions, engagement rate, follower growth per platform. Top posts table sorted by impressions. Platform toggle to filter. Data fetched via /api/v1/social/analytics. Shows loading skeleton while fetching. Empty state when no connected accounts or no data.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see a monthly calendar view of all my scheduled social posts
expected_behaviour: Calendar renders month grid. Each scheduled post appears as a chip on its date showing platform icon and preview text. Clicking a chip opens post detail. Clicking an empty date opens composer preset to that date. Navigation arrows move between months. Today highlighted.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see all my content campaigns and their status
expected_behaviour: Page renders list of campaigns with name, status (Draft/Active/Complete), content count, scheduled range, and created date. New campaign button. Each campaign links to detail. Delete button with confirmation. Empty state when none exist.
errors: Redirect /portal/social/campaigns → /portal/campaigns confirmed working.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to view a campaign's content, share it for client review, and schedule all posts
expected_behaviour: Page renders campaign name, master tagline, asset grid (social posts, blogs, videos). Each asset card shows platform, copy, image, status (Draft/Scheduled/Published). Schedule all button triggers bulk scheduling. Share toggle enables public share link at /c/[token]. Approve/reject per asset. Edit opens composer. Delete removes with confirmation.
errors: Redirect /portal/social/campaigns/:path* → /portal/campaigns/:path* confirmed working.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see @mentions, comments, and DMs from connected social accounts in one inbox
expected_behaviour: Inbox renders list of conversations grouped by account. Each item shows platform icon, profile picture, message preview, time, and read/unread status. Click opens conversation thread with reply input. Reply POSTs to platform via /api/v1/social/reply/[accountId]. Mark as read on open. Filter by platform, read/unread.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to generate social media captions from a brief using AI so I can draft posts quickly
expected_behaviour: Generate modal renders topic/brief input, tone selector (Professional, Casual, Inspirational, etc.), platform selector, and count (1-5 variations). Generate button POSTs to /api/v1/social/generate. Loading spinner shown. Returns text variations displayed as selectable cards. Select one and click Use this to populate composer. Error shown if AI call fails.
errors: Redirect /portal/social/generate → /portal/social/compose confirmed working. Compose is the AI generation entry point.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to use a node-based visual canvas to design and compose social media content workflows
expected_behaviour: Page renders a ReactFlow-based canvas with sidebar showing available node types. Nodes can be dragged onto canvas, connected via edges. Node types include text, image, template, and output nodes. Run button executes the canvas workflow via /api/v1/creative-canvas/[id]/runs/generate. Output panel shows results. Canvas state auto-saved. Load/save named canvases.
errors: Redirect /portal/social/creative-canvas → /portal/creative-canvas confirmed working.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to save and reuse post templates for common content types
expected_behaviour: Templates library shows saved templates with name, platform, preview text, and creation date. Create template button opens form with name, platform, template text (with variable placeholders like {{business_name}}), and tags. Edit and delete per template. Use template button in composer opens template picker.
errors: Email templates hub at /portal/email-templates IS the social templates feature. Redirect confirmed working. Feature fully accessible.

user_story: As a portal user, I want a dashboard showing key CRM metrics at a glance
expected_behaviour: Dashboard renders metric cards: total contacts, new contacts this month, active leads, conversion rate. Recent activity feed shows latest contact events. Quick actions: add contact, send email, create segment. Charts show contact growth over time and source breakdown.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to add and view timestamped notes on a contact record
expected_behaviour: Notes section on contact detail page shows chronological list of notes with author, timestamp, and text. New note textarea at bottom with Save button. Note POSTs to /api/v1/crm/contacts/[id]/notes. Edit and delete per note. Empty state when no notes.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to create automated contact workflows triggered by events like form submission or tag change
expected_behaviour: Workflows list shows all automations with name, trigger, step count, active status toggle, and enrolled count. New workflow button opens builder. Builder renders trigger selector and sequential steps (send email, add tag, wait X days, assign to segment). Save stores workflow. Toggle activates/deactivates. Enrolled contacts shown per workflow.
errors: Redirect /portal/crm/workflows → /portal/settings/automations confirmed working.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to define custom fields for contacts beyond the default fields
expected_behaviour: Custom fields settings page renders list of fields with name, type (text, number, date, boolean, select), and required flag. Add field button opens modal with name, type, and options (for select type). Reorder fields via drag. Delete removes field and warns about data loss. Custom fields appear on contact forms and detail pages.
errors: Redirect /portal/crm/settings/fields → /portal/settings/custom-fields confirmed working.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to manage a suppression list of emails that should never receive marketing messages
expected_behaviour: Page renders list of suppressed emails with reason (unsubscribed, bounced, manual) and date. Add email button manually adds an address. Import CSV for bulk add. Remove button per entry. All email sends check this list before dispatching. Count shown in header.
errors: Redirect /portal/crm/suppression → /portal/email-preferences confirmed working.

user_story: As a portal user, I want contacts who unsubscribe via email link to be automatically suppressed
expected_behaviour: Clicking unsubscribe link in any sent email hits /api/v1/unsubscribe/[token]. Token decoded to contact+org. Contact status set to Unsubscribed, added to suppression list. Renders a branded confirmation page. Re-subscribe link shown. Confirmation email sent to contact.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to publish a post directly to Instagram via the Graph API from the composer
expected_behaviour: When Instagram is selected in composer and user clicks Publish now, calls /api/v1/social/posts with platform=instagram. API creates media container via Graph API, waits for it to be ready, then publishes. On success, post appears in queue with Published status and permalink. On API error shows specific error message (e.g. invalid image ratio, missing permissions).
errors: Instagram publish handled inside /portal/social/compose. Multi-platform support confirmed.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to publish text and image posts to my LinkedIn profile or company page
expected_behaviour: When LinkedIn is selected in composer, share type selector shows Profile vs Company Page (if page is connected). Publish calls /api/v1/social/posts with platform=linkedin. API uses LinkedIn UGC Posts API. On success post appears in queue with Published status. Handles LinkedIn-specific character limits and media requirements.
errors: LinkedIn handled inside composer. Platform type 'linkedin' defined.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to publish tweets including thread support to my Twitter/X account
expected_behaviour: Twitter selected in composer shows 280 character limit per tweet with overflow thread toggle. Thread mode adds + button to add more tweets. Publish calls /api/v1/social/posts with platform=twitter. API uses Twitter v2 API. On success shows tweet URL. Handles rate limits with appropriate error messages.
errors: Twitter/X handled inside composer. Platform type 'twitter'/'x' defined.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see impressions, engagement, and reach for individual published posts
expected_behaviour: Post detail in queue shows metrics panel: impressions, reach, likes, comments, shares, saves, click-through rate. Metrics fetched from /api/v1/social/posts/[id]/metrics which pulls live data from platform APIs. Last synced timestamp shown. Refresh button triggers re-fetch. Metrics chart shows performance over time.
errors: Post performance covered in /portal/social/queue.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to see a unified timeline of all touchpoints with a specific contact
expected_behaviour: Contact detail page includes activity timeline showing: email opens, email clicks, form submissions, page visits, tag changes, notes added, deal stage changes, social interactions. Each event shows icon, description, date/time. Paginated or infinite scroll. Filter by event type.

user_story: As a portal user, I want to select multiple contacts and apply actions like add tag, export, or delete in bulk
